messages.content:
SQL dialect: duckdb

FAILING DEV QUESTIONS (1):
- id: q_net_revenue_all
  question: What is total net revenue?
  verdict: manual_review
  status: UNRESOLVED mismatch — a different result set is not proof of error; the agent's SQL may be equivalent. Only edit context if it is genuinely the agent's fault
  agent_plan: sum all amounts
  generated_sql: SELECT SUM(amount_cents)/100.0 FROM analytics.orders
  gold_sql: SELECT SUM(amount_cents)/100.0 FROM analytics.orders WHERE status='confirmed'

CURRENT PROJECT FILES:
===== semantics/customers.yaml =====
table: analytics.customers
description: One row per customer.
synonyms: [accounts, users]
columns:
  - name: customer_id
    type: bigint
    description: Primary key.
    profile: { nulls: 0.0, distinct: 200, min: 1, max: 200 }
  - name: name
    type: text
    description: Customer display name.
    profile: { nulls: 0.0, distinct: 200 }
  - name: email
    type: text
    description: Contact email.
    profile: { nulls: 0.0, distinct: 200 }
  - name: region
    type: text
    description: Sales region.
    profile: { nulls: 0.0, distinct: 4 }
    sample_values: [us, emea, apac, latam]
  - name: plan
    type: text
    description: Subscription tier.
    profile: { nulls: 0.0, distinct: 3 }
    sample_values: [free, pro, enterprise]
  - name: signup_date
    type: date
    description: Date the customer signed up.
    profile: { nulls: 0.0, min: "2018-06-01", max: "2025-05-31" }
joins:
  - to: analytics.orders
    type: one_to_many
    on: "customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id"

===== semantics/orders.yaml =====
table: analytics.orders
description: One row per confirmed order. Excludes carts and draft orders.
synonyms:
- purchases
- transactions
- sales
columns:
- name: order_id
  type: bigint
  description: Primary key.
  profile:
    nulls: 0.0
    distinct: 2000
    min: 1
    max: 2000
- name: customer_id
  type: bigint
  description: FK to analytics.customers.customer_id.
  profile:
    nulls: 0.0
    distinct: 200
    min: 1
    max: 200
- name: amount_cents
  type: bigint
  description: Order total in cents. Divide by 100 for dollars.
  profile:
    nulls: 0.0
    min: 127
    max: 310229
    p50: 5011
    p95: 31000
- name: status
  type: text
  description: One of 'confirmed','refunded','partial_refund'.
  profile:
    nulls: 0.0
    distinct: 3
  sample_values:
  - confirmed
  - partial_refund
  - refunded
- name: created_at
  type: timestamp
  description: Order confirmation time (UTC).
  profile:
    nulls: 0.0
    min: '2019-02-02'
    max: '2026-06-28'
joins:
- to: analytics.customers
  type: many_to_one
  'on': orders.customer_id = customers.customer_id
filters:
- name: last_quarter
  sql: created_at >= date_trunc('quarter', now()) - interval '3 months'

===== examples/revenue.yaml =====
- question: What was net revenue last month?
  sql: |
    SELECT SUM(amount_cents)/100.0 AS net_revenue
    FROM analytics.orders
    WHERE status='confirmed'
      AND created_at >= date_trunc('month', now()) - interval '1 month'
      AND created_at <  date_trunc('month', now());
  tags: [revenue, time-window]

- question: How many orders were refunded?
  sql: |
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS refunded_orders
    FROM analytics.orders
    WHERE status='refunded';
  tags: [orders, status]

- question: What is net revenue by region?
  sql: |
    SELECT c.region,
           SUM(CASE WHEN o.status='confirmed' THEN o.amount_cents ELSE 0 END)/100.0 AS net_revenue
    FROM analytics.orders o
    JOIN analytics.customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
    GROUP BY c.region
    ORDER BY net_revenue DESC;
  tags: [revenue, join, group-by]

===== trusted/mrr.sql =====
-- @name: monthly_recurring_revenue
-- @params: month (date)
-- @description: Official monthly net revenue. Prefer this over ad-hoc revenue math.
SELECT SUM(amount_cents) / 100.0 AS net_revenue
FROM analytics.orders
WHERE status = 'confirmed'
  AND created_at >= date_trunc('month', :month)
  AND created_at <  date_trunc('month', :month) + interval '1 month';

===== instructions.md =====
# Instructions

This database tracks customer orders. Keep answers grounded in the semantic layer.

- "Revenue" means **net revenue**: confirmed orders only, refunds excluded. Prefer
  the `net_revenue` measure over ad-hoc arithmetic.
- Monetary columns are stored in **cents**; divide by 100 to report dollars.
- All timestamps are UTC. Relative windows ("last month") are relative to `now()`.
- Prefer the `monthly_recurring_revenue` trusted asset for any MRR question.


Propose the ranked, minimal file diffs that fix the most failures. Return them all.

_ProposalDraft.description: One proposal as the model returns it (before it gets a stable id).
_ProposalDraft.title: _ProposalDraft

response_schema.description: The structured result of the single paid Coach call.
response_schema.title: _CoachDraft

request.system:
You are the Coach for a text-to-SQL agent. You improve the agent by editing its project files — never by editing SQL by hand. You are shown the questions the agent got wrong on its DEV benchmark (its plan, its generated SQL, the gold answer, the scorer verdicts, and any error) and the agent's CURRENT project files.

Your job: cluster the failures by ROOT CAUSE, then for each cluster propose the MINIMAL, highest-leverage edit at the RIGHT layer of this hierarchy (most preferred first):
  1. examples/     — a worked question→SQL example (sets the accuracy ceiling)
  2. trusted/      — a curated, blessed SQL asset
  3. semantics/    — a measure, a synonym, a named filter, or a column/table description
  4. instructions.md — a GLOBAL PROSE rule (LAST RESORT: prose conflicts and generalizes poorly; never reach for it when a measure, synonym, description, or example would fix it)

Rules: (1) prefer data/examples over prose; (2) keep every edit minimal and focused; (3) flag any conflict an edit could introduce with existing metadata; (4) rank proposals by leverage — how many failing questions each fixes and how confident you are; (5) each proposal MUST be a UNIFIED DIFF against exactly one named project file (relative path), so it can be applied and reverted with git. Give predicted_fixes and a confidence in [0,1].

GENERALIZE, don't memorize. When several failures share a root cause, prefer ONE general fix (a measure, synonym, description, or named filter) that covers the whole cluster. Add an examples/ entry only when it teaches a reusable PATTERN that will also help questions you have NOT been shown — never an example that merely restates the gold SQL of a single failing question. Copying one question's gold into an example inflates the dev score without generalizing to the held-out set; that is training on the benchmark, not fixing the agent.

A mismatch is NOT proof the agent is wrong. Some failing rows are UNRESOLVED — the generated SQL may be semantically equivalent to the gold, or the gold itself may be wrong. Each row is labelled with its status. For an unresolved row where the agent's SQL looks correct, do NOT contort the agent's context to match a questionable gold — return no proposal for it and let a human review the benchmark. Only propose context edits for failures that are genuinely the agent's fault.

proposals.diff:
--- a/semantics/orders.yaml
+++ b/semantics/orders.yaml
@@ measures @@
+measures:
+  - name: net_revenue
+    description: Revenue net of refunds.
+    sql: "SUM(CASE WHEN status='confirmed' THEN amount_cents ELSE 0 END)/100.0"
proposals.rationale: A reusable measure fixes every revenue question at the semantics layer.
proposals.root_cause: the agent summed amount_cents without excluding refunds
proposals.title: Add measure net_revenue to semantics/orders.yaml
